name: Mansilla Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de Mansilla, Viniegra de Abajo – ruta circular
description: This difficult 15.8-mile circular hike around Embalse de Mansilla offers panoramic views and historical insights in La Rioja.
region_intro: Hiking around Viniegra De Abajo offers diverse natural landscapes nestled between the Demanda and Urbión mountain ranges in La Rioja, Spain. The region is characterized by wild terrain, including the Urbión River, which flows through the village, and various forests of oak and pine. Hikers can explore a mix of challenging mountain paths and serene river valleys, with elevations ranging from gentle slopes to significant climbs. Best hiking trails around Viniegra De Abajo The most popular hiking route is Pico de Urbión and the Source of the Duero River Loop, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the high mountain landscape around the source of the Duero River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mansilla Reservoir Loop, a difficult 15.8 miles (25.4 km) path. This extensive loop offers panoramic views around the Embalse de Mansilla. Local hikers also love the Monasterio de Valvanera loop from Monasterio de Valvanera, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through forested areas, often completed in about 1 hour 29 minutes. Hiking around Viniegra De Abajo is defined by mountain ranges, river valleys, and diverse forests, including oak and pine. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Viniegra De Abajo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 180 reviews. More than 2100 hikers have used komoot to explore Viniegra De Abajo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Viniegra De Abajo?
A: There are over 75 hiking trails around Viniegra De Abajo, offering a wide range of options from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. The komoot community has explored more than 2100 of these routes.

Q: Are there easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Viniegra De Abajo?
A: Yes, Viniegra De Abajo offers several easy hikes perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10463860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Valvanera loop from Monasterio de Valvanera&lt;/a&gt; is a 3.1-mile (5.0 km) trail through forested areas, typically completed in under 1.5 hours.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Viniegra De Abajo?
A: The region around Viniegra De Abajo is characterized by wild and diverse natural landscapes. You'll encounter dramatic mountain ranges like the Demanda and Urbión, serene river valleys carved by the Urbión River, and extensive forests of oak and pine. The area also features natural lagoons and high mountain terrain.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Viniegra De Abajo?
A: Yes, there are many circular routes available. A notable challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989591919/mansilla-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mansilla Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 15.8-mile (25.4 km) path offering panoramic views around the Embalse de Mansilla. For a moderate circular hike, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058968468/pico-de-urbion-and-the-source-of-the-duero-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión and the Source of the Duero River Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Hikers can discover several significant natural attractions. You might encounter the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3328794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Duero River&lt;/a&gt;, or the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga&lt;/a&gt;. The area also boasts charming villages like Viniegra de Abajo itself, recognized as one of Spain's most beautiful.

Q: Are there any waterfalls in the Viniegra De Abajo area?
A: Yes, the region is home to several picturesque waterfalls. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4704240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojeda River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the Paso Peñoncito Waterfall and the Cascada de la Chorrera, which provide beautiful spots for hikers to enjoy.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Viniegra De Abajo?
A: The diverse terrain of Viniegra De Abajo makes it suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, while summer is ideal for exploring higher mountain trails. Winter can provide opportunities for snowshoeing or more challenging hikes, especially in the Sierra de la Demanda.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Viniegra De Abajo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 180 reviews. Reviewers often praise the wild, astonishing beauty of the landscapes, the variety of trails for different skill levels, and the serene river valleys and forests.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers, there are numerous challenging routes, particularly in the Demanda and Urbión mountain ranges. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Yews of Urbión – Laguna de Urbión loop from Tramborríos&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains and longer distances, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Viniegra De Abajo?
A: Many trails in the natural areas around Viniegra De Abajo are generally dog-friendly, allowing you to explore with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to check specific trail regulations or local signage, especially within protected areas, and to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Viniegra De Abajo?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in or near Viniegra De Abajo village, which serves as a common starting point for many trails. For specific trailheads further afield, there are often designated parking areas, though it's advisable to check the route details on komoot for precise parking information.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for hiking in the Sierra de la Demanda or Urbión mountains?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for day hiking on established trails in the Sierra de la Demanda or Urbión mountains around Viniegra De Abajo. However, for certain activities like camping or accessing very sensitive ecological zones, it's always best to consult local park authorities for any specific regulations that might apply.
tips: In addition to the church of the Assumption, this town has a rural house and beautiful houses belonging to families who returned from Latin America with a lot of money and who they called "los indianos". Cool off in the Cuatro Caños fountain.
The Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción is a construction made of ashlar masonry and reddish stone, characteristic of the area.

The Mansilla reservoir is located on the Najerilla river, a tributary of the Ebro river. Its waters hide the old town of Mansilla de la Sierra, whose church and some other buildings are visible when it is low.

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for the Embalse de Mansilla, Viniegra de Abajo circular route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is classified as a "Hard hike" and requires very good fitness. Hikers should also be sure-footed, as some sections may be uneven or slightly technical.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses the mountainous landscape of La Rioja, offering a diverse mix of surfaces. You can expect old paths, traditional agricultural land, ravines, and various forest types including oak, Scots pine, and beech. While generally accessible, some sections require sure-footedness.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Embalse de Mansilla circular route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable sights. You'll pass through the charming settlement of Viniegra de Abajo, and can see the Church of Our Lady of the Assumption. A major highlight is the Mansilla Reservoir itself, with panoramic views of its water surface and the new village of Mansilla in the distance. When water levels are low, the ruins of the old submerged village of Mansilla de la Sierra become visible, offering a unique historical perspective.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, it is generally recommended to look for parking options in or near Viniegra de Abajo, which serves as a starting point for this circular route. Alternatively, parking might be available closer to the Mansilla Reservoir.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Embalse de Mansilla, Viniegra de Abajo circular route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is typically during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. If you wish to see the ruins of the submerged village of Mansilla, visiting during periods of lower water levels (often late summer or early autumn) would be ideal.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this trail?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact route are not detailed, trails in natural areas of La Rioja generally permit dogs. It is always advisable to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety and comfort of other hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to hike in Parque Natural del Alto Najerilla?
faqs.answer: The Embalse de Mansilla, Viniegra de Abajo circular route is located within the Parque Natural del Alto Najerilla. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking on established trails within this natural park. However, always check local regulations before your visit, especially for any seasonal restrictions or protected areas.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this extensive circular route incorporates sections of several other trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Sendero del Portilla, the Embalse de Mansilla trail, and the Sendero de la Ermita de Santiago, among others. It also briefly touches upon the Altos Valles Ibéricos and Camino de Altuzarra a Mansilla de la sierra.

faqs.question: What unique historical feature can I observe on this hike?
faqs.answer: A poignant historical feature is the old village of Mansilla de la Sierra, which was submerged by the Mansilla Reservoir in 1960. During periods of low water levels, the ruins of the old church and other structures of this submerged village become visible, offering a unique glimpse into the past.

faqs.question: Is there any public transport available to reach the starting point of the trail?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to remote mountainous areas like Viniegra de Abajo can be limited. It is recommended to check local bus schedules for services to the village, but a private vehicle is often the most convenient way to access the trailhead for this route.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter along the route?
faqs.answer: The diverse natural environment of La Rioja, including its forests and mountainous terrain, supports various wildlife. While specific sightings are never guaranteed, you might spot local bird species, small mammals, and insects. The region is known for its natural beauty, so keep an eye out for the flora and fauna.

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of the Assumption
